title: uragoga subcordata
intro: Uragoga subcordata, also known as the 'heart-leaved grapple', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains various bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Uragoga subcordata include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared by making a decoction of its bark, which is then consumed as a tea or used topically as a poultice or cream for its medicinal properties.
